messages.content: You answer questions about the Bible. When the user asks what a verse says, use the lookup_verse tool to get its exact text, then answer quoting only the text the tool returned.

function.name: lookup_verse
function.description: Look up the exact text of a Bible verse by reference, e.g. "John 3:16".

message.thinking:
The user asks: "What does John 3:16 say?" According to policy, we must use the lookup_verse tool to get exact text. So we need to call lookup_verse with ref "John 3:16". Then answer quoting only the text returned.

Thus:

[analysis]
We need to retrieve verse text.

messages.content: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

function.name: lookup_verse
function.description: Look up the exact text of a Bible verse by reference, e.g. "John 3:16".

message.content: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
message.thinking:
The user asks: "What does John 3:16 say?" We used lookup_verse tool and got the exact text. According to the tool response:

"For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life."

We need to answer quoting only the text the tool returned. So we output exactly that sentence.

Thus final answer: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.

Make sure no extra commentary.
